name: Republic of Texas (1836–1839)
description: An azure (dark blue) field bearing a single large gold five-pointed star in the centre — the 'Burnet flag'.
history: The first national flag of the Republic of Texas, known as the 'Burnet flag' after President David G. Burnet. Adopted on 10 December 1836, it bore a single gold star on an azure field. It was superseded as the national flag by the Lone Star flag on 25 January 1839, though it continued in use as the Republic's war flag until 1845.

features.role: the golden star of the Burnet flag
